items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

gallery.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

thumbnail.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, despite objection of owners

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

image.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

image.altText: Cars fill a freeway

items.title: Rain easing for AFL Gather Round as negotiations for event's future continue

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology says wet weather conditions will improve for AFL Gather Round matches as the weekend progresses, as the premier shares his ongoing bid to keep the event in South Australia.

articles.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in frantic finish

children.content: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval.

image.altText: Jye Amiss flexes his arms during the Dockers' AFL win over the Magpies.

articles.title: SA premier wants 'certainty' beyond a one-year Gather Round deal

children.content: The South Australian premier is pushing for the state to be awarded a long-term contract for Gather Round, but says it could take weeks before a deal about the future of the footy festival is struck.

image.altText: Both men smiling in front of media microphones at a playground

gallery.altText: A man and a boy wearing Adelaide Crows jerseys make fists in the air outside a stadium at night
Blurry figures walk past a statue into a stadium
A man and a boy wearing Adelaide Crows jerseys make fists in the air outside a stadium at night

A man and a boy wearing Adelaide Crows jerseys make fists in the air outside a stadium at night

items.title: SA GP with an 'unhealthy obsession' for laser therapy disqualified

children.content: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

image.altText: Close-up photo of a silver and white stethoscope. Dec 2022.

items.title: 'Preferred profit … over the environment': Farm owner fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland

children.content: The owner of a farm in South Australia's south-east is convicted and fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland, but the penalty is much less than the $2.2 million he would have had to pay for a permit for the work.

image.altText: A small bird with grey and gold plumage with a black chin

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

gallery.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

thumbnail.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

items.title: Four charged for alleged offences 'connected' to murder of Adelaide father Chris Robertson

children.content: Four people have been arrested by police for alleged offences "connected" to the alleged murder of Adelaide father-of-two Chris Robertson last year.

image.altText: A photo of Chris Robertson whose death is being treated as murder.

items.title: Trucks about to get longer, heavier in bid to reduce fuel costs

children.content: The South Australian government says recent changes to heavy vehicle freight limits will lower fuel costs and reduce the number of freight trucks needed on the road.

image.altText: an image of a car's driver side mirror with a truck reflected, driving through a town

items.title: Festival chief warned author's removal would 'decimate' Writers' Week

children.content: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

image.altText: Emails obtained under FOI relating to Adelaide Writers' Week.

items.title: Mistrial in alleged murder case after juror researched perjury

children.content: A trial of four defendants charged in connection to the alleged murder of Geoffrey McLean — whose body was found dumped in a wheelie bin in Adelaide's north — has been vacated after a juror conducted independent research.

image.altText: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Amid 'unprecedented' times, farmers take 'risky' gamble on early winter crops

children.content: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

image.altText: A drone shot of dry seeding in a paddock near Jamestown, in SA's mid north in May 2025

items.title: War hero's looming prosecution divides opinion

children.content: Once celebrated as Australia's most decorated living war hero, the looming prosecution of Ben Roberts-Smith is shaping up to be the next trial of the century.

image.altText: a man leaning against a wall which holds a portrait of him wearing an army uniform

items.title: Artemis II crew prepares to ride 'fireball' back to Earth

children.content: Here's a breakdown of how the Orion space capsule copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

gallery.altText: NASA Orion space capsule floats in the ocean with orange balloons and a navy ship in the background.
The Orion space capsule floats over the ocean, slowed down by balloons.

thumbnail.altText: NASA Orion space capsule floats in the ocean with orange balloons and a navy ship in the background.
The Orion space capsule floats over the ocean, slowed down by balloons.

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: A look at the sea mines Iran claims are in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

image.altText: A smartphone screen shows traffic in Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UK charity sues Prince Harry for defamation

children.content: Prince Harry co-founded Sentebale 20 years ago in honour of his late mother, Princess Diana, but now the organisation is suing him for defamation.

image.altText: Prince Harry smiles in front of Sentebale branding, wearing a khaki button-up shirt

items.title: Hull 'robbed' at national champs as rival disqualified after 1,500m clash

children.content: Jess Hull had been on track to claim a fourth-straight 1,500m national title before being clipped by Claudia Hollingsworth, bringing her reign crashing down.

image.altText: Runner Jess Hull falls and has arms splayed out in front of her

items.title: 'Really big deal': P-pop group to make history at Coachella

children.content: As one of the world's largest music festivals gets underway this weekend, a pop group from the Philippines will make history.

gallery.altText: A group of girls on stage hold microphones and appear to be singing.

thumbnail.altText: A group of girls on stage hold microphones and appear to be singing.

items.title: Alex de Minaur downed by local hero at Monte Carlo Masters

children.content: Alex de Minaur will slip from his career-high ranking after missing a chance to emulate his semifinal run at the Monte Carlo Masters, beaten in three sets by Monaco's Valentin Vacherot.

image.altText: Alex de Minaur completes his forehand swing over his shoulder during a tennis match.

items.title: How the long bomb is making an impact this AFL season

children.content: In a league where inside-50s are so often king, teams with prowess from outside the arc are making a major impact to start the 2026 AFL season.

image.altText: Willem Duursma kicks from outside 50m

items.title: 'Cosplay is not consent': The rules of dressing up for conventions

children.content: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman dressed in costume

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of a classic poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: A cartoon cat and mouse sit on rocking chairs on a porch. Lemonade is on a table between them

items.title: Debra Adelaide's best friend died by suicide. She wrote a novel about her

children.content: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

image.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

items.title: Small oil refinery in outback Queensland could be on cusp of expansion

children.content: The Inland Oil Refinery produces specialised underground mining fuel, heating oils and other speciality solvents, and supplies a small amount of on-road diesel for the local community

gallery.altText: aerial of oil refinery and truck

thumbnail.altText: aerial of oil refinery and truck
